title: Parameterized Access, Authorization, Privacy, and Audit · Mastery Check
summary: A web service accepts user filters, exposes support data by role, and must resist injection and excessive collection. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a least-privilege parameterized data-access contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify code-data boundary failures in dynamic SQL and demonstrate why escaping alone is incomplete.
Bind untrusted values through prepared statements while allow-listing unavoidable identifiers or directions.
Design separate identities, roles, grants, views, and row or column controls for minimum required authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

quiz case — A viewer submits severity text "high' OR 1=1 --" and sort direction "desc" while requesting member details.
